content:
# elizaos/eliza Weekly Report (Jun 21 - 27, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
The week of June 21–27 was defined by a rigorous push toward framework hardening, infrastructure stability, and a major model transition. The team successfully implemented mandatory E2E testing gates, unified file management across all supported platforms, and completed a strategic cutover to the Gemma 4 model line. These efforts, combined with new cloud-native security and monetization features, significantly improve the reliability and production-readiness of the Eliza agent framework.

## 🛠️ Key Developments

### Testing and Quality Assurance
*   **E2E Hardening:** A major milestone was reached with the implementation of a mandatory E2E ship-gate, ensuring all plugin routes and commands are verified before merging ([#8816](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8816), [#8846](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8846)). UI-smoke test specs were also realigned to match current product designs ([#8830](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8830)).
*   **Coding Capability Coverage:** The team introduced extensive testing for coding orchestration and platform-specific live-model interactions ([#8974](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8974), [#8955](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8955)).

### Model & Inference Optimization
*   **Gemma 4 Transition:** The `eliza-1` local model stack was migrated from Qwen to Gemma 4, consolidating the local inference architecture ([#9060](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9060)).
*   **Performance:** Development focused on optimizing cold-boot times and parallelizing document-recovery searches to reduce latency ([#8836](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8836), [#8850](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8850)). Discussions regarding on-device inference highlighted a shift toward fused kernels and MTP speculative decoding to bypass hardware-specific limitations.

### Agent Orchestration & File Management
*   **Unified File Handling:** A comprehensive initiative standardized file and attachment operations across all connectors (Telegram, WhatsApp, Nostr, etc.), including new CRUD operations and 3D model preview support ([#8876](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8876), [#8956](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8956)).
*   **Orchestration:** New "grilling" loops and TaskSupervisor services were implemented to improve sub-agent verification and swarm visibility ([#8950](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8950), [#9013](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9013)).

### Cloud & Monetization Infrastructure
*   **Financials:** Core infrastructure for Stripe Connect fiat payouts and durable per-session spend ledgers was established ([#9041](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9041), [#9032](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9032)).
*   **Security & Ingress:** The team prioritized cloud security by isolating untrusted app builds and refining ingress configurations for node-local Caddy routing ([#9230](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9230), [#9229](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9229)).

## 🐛 Issues & Triage

### Closed Issues
*   **Quality Assurance:** The comprehensive E2E coverage sweep was successfully closed ([#8802](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8802)), transitioning the coverage gate from advisory to a mandatory requirement.
*   **Infrastructure:** Several issues related to session spend persistence and model cutovers were resolved via PR-linked closures ([#8924](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8924), [#8808](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8808)).

### New & Active Issues
*   **LifeOps & Inference:** New issues were opened regarding the completion of the live owner/agent connector ([#8833](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8833)) and the enforcement of specific model optimizations like TurboQuant and KV-quant to meet strict performance constraints ([#8848](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8848)).
*   **Blockers:** Product-2 Blocker #6 (related to untrusted app build isolation) was identified as a priority for the cloud infrastructure team.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
The project demonstrated high velocity in collaborative development, particularly in the "coding-capability" and "orchestration" workstreams. The high volume of PRs related to cross-connector support and cloud-agent handoffs suggests a coordinated effort to unify the user experience across diverse platforms. The documentation of token-by-token streaming via video evidence ([#9209](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9209)) indicates a strong focus on transparency and providing clear technical proof-of-concept for the community.
